data.title: Gold, silver end higher after U.S. reports weaker than expected jobs data
data.description:
Gold and silver gained Friday after disappointing U.S. labor market data added to bullish momentum, with non-farm payrolls rising by a smaller than expected 50K in December while the unemployment rate eased to 4.4% and wages increased by 0.3%.

Analysts say the gold market is becoming increasingly comfortable with the idea of a cooling labor market and a Fed that would not need to tighten further.

"The combination of weaker job creation, a dip in the unemployment rate and a softer dollar has given gold fresh support, and while this doesn’t guarantee an immediate retest of record highs, it reinforces the broader uptrend," Neil Welsh at Britannia Global Markets said in a note. "Sticky inflation may slow the pace of rate cuts, but it's unlikely to stop them altogether, meaning real-yield pressure on gold should continue to ease."

Geopolitical tensions also remain elevated with intensifying unrest in Iran, continued fighting in Russia's war in Ukraine, the U.S. capture of Venezuela's President Maduro, and the Trump administration's talk of taking control of Greenland.

Gold prices could hit fresh record highs above $5,000 in 2026, citing de-dollarization trends and geopolitical risks, precious metals consultancy Metals Focus said.

Gold and silver futures both posted their second-highest all-time settlement Friday, with front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery +0.9% to $4,490.30/oz and the front-month Comex January silver contract (XAGUSD:CUR) +5.6% to $78.884/oz; for the week, gold gained 4.1% and silver surged 11.8%.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: FluroTech appoints Reem Chalhoub as CFO
data.description:
• FluroTech (TEST.H:CA) on Friday is pleased to announce that effective October 6, 2025, Reem Chalhoub has been appointed as the new Chief Financial Officer.
  • Chalhoub succeeds Curtis Smith, who has departed the company. 
  • Chalhoub started her career at PwC, where she worked with corporations such as Bayer and Ford. She later served as the Head of Corporate Finance at Ecolab Canada. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vasta Platform Limited announces delisting from NASDAQ
data.description:
• Vasta Platform Limited (VSTA) on Friday said it has notified Nasdaq of its decision to voluntarily delist its Class A common shares from the Nasdaq Global Select Market.
  • The move follows Cogna Educação’s acquisition of 97.2% of Vasta’s shares through a tender offer disclosed in December.
  • Vasta’s board approved the delisting on January 8, citing costs associated with being a U.S.-listed company, limited public float and an illiquid market for its shares.
  • Vasta said it plans to file a Form 25 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission around January 19, with the last trading day on Nasdaq expected to be around January 29.
  • The company also intends to file a Form 15 before the end of January to suspend its U.S. reporting obligations.
  • VSTA -5.17% after hours to $4.76.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stellantis ends production of PHEV Jeep SUVs and Chrysler minivan -- report
data.description:
Another automaker is scaling back its EV ambitions with Stellantis (STLA) ending production of its existing plug-in hybrid ((PHEV)) models including the Jeep Grand Cherokee,Jeep Wrangler, and Chrysler Pacifica, according to an emailed statement to CNBC on Friday.

The decision was based on soft customer demand and the need to focus on more popular hybrids and range-extended vehicles. Stellantis’ PHEVs have also been plagued by reliability issues as multiple recalls have been issued to address fire risks and powertrain malfunctions.

“With customer demand shifting, Stellantis will phase out plug‑in hybrid (PHEV) programs in North America beginning with the 2026 model year,” Stellantis said in their email.

The decision comes on the heels of back-to-back announcements from Ford (F) and General Motors (GM) to take substantial write-offs to downsize their EV programs to compensate for lackluster demand, an end to government subsidies, and easing emission standards in both the U.S. and Europe.  

The company will continue with production of a hybrid Jeep Cherokee, range-extended electric Jeep Grand Wagoneer, and Ram 1500 REV pickup truck.

data.title: Equinor warns it could terminate Empire Wind project if Trump order stands
data.description:
Delaying construction of the Empire Wind project beyond January 16 would mean the project off the coast of New York "faces likely termination due to disruption of a tightly choreographed construction schedule" and create an "existential threat" for its financing, Equinor (EQNR) said in a court filing this week, Bloomberg reported Friday.

A complete loss of the project due to the Trump administration's recent order to halt construction could cost the company ~$5.3 billion, including costs to dismantle and scrap parts and mothball facilities, the filing said, adding that the project is more than 60% complete.

Equinor (EQNR) said the harm "became more acute" on January 5, when the Trump administration said the company would not be allowed to carry out construction work linked to essential safety-related activities, adding the factors weighing on the schedule includes contracts with two specialized vessels, which have subsequent commitments that will make them unavailable for years.

data.title: Craig Fullalove to succeed Julie Cameron-Doe as CFO at Wynn Resorts
data.description:
• Wynn Resorts (WYNN) said on Friday that Craig Fullalove will succeed Julie Cameron-Doe as chief financial officer of Wynn Resorts.
  • Cameron-Doe has elected to retire in mid-2026 but will continue to serve as a consultant to the company and as a Non-Executive Director of Wynn Macau, the company said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Webuy Global receives Nasdaq notice over minimum equity shortfall
data.description:
• Webuy Global (WBUY) said on Friday it has received a notice from Nasdaq stating the company is not in compliance with the exchange’s minimum stockholders’ equity requirement for continued listing.
  • The Singapore-based company said it reported stockholders’ equity of $364,584 as of June 30, 2025, below Nasdaq’s $2.5 million minimum. Its shares continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market and the notice has no immediate impact on its listing status.
  • Webuy said it has 45 days, until February 19, 2026, to submit a plan to regain compliance. If accepted, Nasdaq may grant up to 180 days to meet the requirement.
  • The company said it is evaluating measures to address the deficiency.
  • WBUY closed -7.04% at $1.32.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vasta Platform announces delisting from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Vasta Platform (NASDAQ:VSTA) announced  on Friday that it had notified the Nasdaq on January 8, 2026, of its decision to voluntarily delist its Class A Common Shares, par value $0.00005 per share, from the Nasdaq Global Select Market.
  • Cogna Educação had acquired 97.2% of the company’s outstanding shares on December 11, 2025, through its tender offer and intends to delist Vasta’s shares from Nasdaq.
  • Vasta expects that the last trading day of its shares on the Nasdaq Global Select Market will be on or about January 29, 2026.

data.title: LendingTree promotes SVP to COO
data.description:
• LendingTree (TREE) promoted Ian Smith to chief operating officer.
  • Smith previously led LendingTree’s insurance business as senior vice president.
  • Laura Nelson will step into the role of Head of Insurance following Smith’s transition.
  • Scott Peyree, former COO, now serves as CEO after the passing of founder Doug Lebda.

data.title: K Wave Media receives Nasdaq notification
data.description:
• K Wave Media (NASDAQ:KWM) on Friday  received a notice from Nasdaq on Jan. 7, 2026, stating that the company is not in compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2) after its shares traded below the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement from Nov. 20, 2025, through Jan. 6, 2026.
  • Pursuant to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5810(c)(3)(A), the company has a compliance period of 180 calendar days, or until July 6, 2026, to regain compliance. 
  • The company is currently evaluating options to timely regain compliance with Nasdaq’s continued listing requirement.
